reasons: Lolita protects Layla from ranged projectiles and gives her a front wall to attack behind.
Layla can use her range more safely when Lolita blocks incoming pressure from the front.

proof.summary: Lolita blocks ranged pressure while Layla uses her range to keep dealing damage from the backline.
worksBestWhen: Lolita positions between Layla and enemy poke.
Layla keeps attacking from behind Lolita instead of stepping forward.
failureCases: Layla is caught while Lolita's shield is unavailable.

reasons: Minotaur gives Layla frontline protection, healing, and crowd control against enemies trying to dive her.
Layla can keep attacking when Minotaur peels divers away from her backline position.

proof.summary: Minotaur protects Layla by standing in front, healing, and controlling divers who enter her attack space.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur saves control for enemy dive timing.
failureCases: Enemy assassins attack Layla from an unprotected side angle.

reasons: Rafaela helps Layla reposition, survive poke, and escape when enemies try to close the distance.
Layla benefits from heal and speed support because her damage pattern depends on keeping safe spacing.

proof.summary: Rafaela gives Layla healing and speed support, improving Layla's ability to keep safe spacing.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela saves speed support for dive or disengage.
Layla stays close enough to receive support.
failureCases: Layla is burst before Rafaela can heal.
Layla overextends after receiving speed support.

reasons: Tigreal creates space in front of Layla and can start fights that allow Layla to fire safely from range.
Layla can follow Tigreal's control from a protected backline position.

proof.summary: Tigreal holds the frontline and controls enemies, giving Layla time and space to attack from the backline.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages within Layla's follow-up range.
Layla does not walk forward into enemy counter-engage.
failureCases: Tigreal dives too deep and leaves Layla unprotected.
Enemy flankers ignore Tigreal and reach Layla.

reasons: Diggie can protect Layla from crowd-control engage, which is one of the main ways enemies punish her immobility.
Layla can keep attacking when Diggie reduces the value of enemy lockdown timing.

proof.summary: Diggie reduces the threat of crowd-control engage, helping Layla keep attacking instead of being locked down immediately.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves protection for the enemy's real engage.
Layla stays near Diggie during key teamfights.
failureCases: Diggie's key protection is baited early.
Layla is killed by raw burst before protection matters.
